_schema.description: Curated 'situation -> reaction' case library for PSX. Read by brain/playbook.py at every Master Strategist run: the engine matches today's active drivers / regime / flow regime against case 'trigger_signals', returns the top-K most-relevant cases, and injects them under an 'analogues' section in the strategist briefing. Claude is required (by system prompt) to either name the analogue it is leaning on or explain why none fits.

fields.id: Stable identifier (snake_case). Used by retrospective tooling.
fields.category: Bucket: macro_event | sector_event | flow_regime | behavioural | seasonal.
fields.title: One-line human label.
fields.trigger_signals:
List of structured selectors the matcher evaluates against the live briefing. Supported selectors:
   * 'driver:<tag>'                 fires when macro_impact emits the tag (e.g. 'driver:circular_debt_resolution', 'driver:rate_down').
   * 'driver:<tag>:STRONG'           same, but only at STRONG magnitude.
   * 'regime:<NAME>'                 fires when get_market_regime returns the named regime (NORMAL/CAUTION/CRISIS).
   * 'fipi_5d_lt:<num>'              fires when fipi_flows.net_5d_pkr_mn is BELOW the threshold (negative numbers OK).
   * 'fipi_5d_gt:<num>'              fires when fipi_flows.net_5d_pkr_mn is ABOVE the threshold.
   * 'sentiment_lt:<num>'            fires when scored_sentiment.tilt is below the threshold (range [-1, +1]).
   * 'breadth_lt:<num>'              fires when regime.breadth_pct_up is below the threshold.
   * 'universe_5d_lt:<num>'          fires when regime.universe_ret_5d is below the threshold (e.g. -0.05).
   * 'sector:<Name>'                 sector context (qualifies the case but doesn't trigger on its own; used for ranking).
   * 'phase1:<CASH|RISK_ON>'         fires on the strategy_signal market_risk_on flag.
fields.min_triggers: How many 'trigger_signals' must fire for the case to be returned. Default = all of them. Set to 1 for OR semantics, set to 2-3 for partial-match patterns.
fields.historical_instances: List of past occurrences. Each item has 'date', optional 'context', and a 'reactions' map of symbol/sector -> {d1, d5, d21} fractional returns (NOT percentages). Sources should be cited.
fields.playbook: What to do today, given the analogue. Concrete: which sectors / names to lean into or away from, suggested time horizon, position sizing nudges. Keep it 2-5 sentences.
fields.what_breaks_it: When the analogue stops applying. Critical for honesty.
fields.confidence: HIGH (mechanism + n>=3 instances) | MEDIUM (mechanism + n>=1 OR strong analytical case) | LOW (analytical only).
fields.research_basis: Citations: 'Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx' section, public reports, etc.
fields.tags: Free-form labels for the UI / retrospective miner.
fields.reactions: OPTIONAL deterministic action recipe applied by brain/strategist_overlays.py AFTER the LLM (or fallback) produces actions. Schema: {cash_floor_pct, sector_overlay:{sector:downgrade_one|upgrade_one}, symbol_overlay:{sym:{min_bucket,max_bucket,weight_floor_pct}}, conviction_cap, position_size_multiplier, narrative_note}. Bucket order: AVOID<TRIM<WATCH<HOLD<ADD<BUY.

_schema.how_to_add_a_case: Append a new object to 'cases'. Re-running the Master Strategist immediately picks it up. Cases are evaluated independently and ranked by (a) number of trigger matches, (b) recency of historical instances, (c) confidence band. The matcher returns the top 4 by default; tune via PSX_PLAYBOOK_TOP_K env var.
_schema.auto_mining_status: Layer-3 retrospective auto-mining is NOT active yet — wait for >=6 months of Master Strategist run history. Until then the file is hand-curated. See README -> 'The Playbook (institutional memory)' for the rationale.
_schema.validation: Run `python -m brain.playbook --validate` to type-check the file (also runs in CI on touched cases.json files).
_schema.research_doc: Most case patterns trace back to 'Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx' (sections: Macroeconomic Determinants, Behavioural Mechanics, Sectoral Sensitivities).

cases.title: Power-sector circular debt cleared via banking syndicate (>= Rs 500bn) — buy-the-news, first 7 days only
cases.pattern: Government brokers a large structured settlement of power-sector circular debt, typically via a commercial-bank syndicate refinancing IPP receivables. Frees IPP cash flow, materially improves bank loan-book risk profile on legacy power exposure, and second-order benefits OMCs (PSO is the largest single creditor) and E&Ps (Mari, OGDC, PPL) whose receivables stretch shortens. **Tier-0 patch (2026-05-03):** the underlying `circular_debt_resolution_event` decay was shortened from 60 to 7 days so the case fires only on the **fresh announcement window** (the first 5-7 trading days). Beyond that the news is fully priced in; the trader still HOLDS the position 30-45 days per the playbook below, but the SYSTEM stops re-firing the case every Mon/Wed/Fri.

historical_instances.context: Rs 1.225 trillion ($4.29bn) clearance backed by 18 commercial banks. Largest banking transaction in Pakistan's history. Restructured Rs 660bn of legacy loans + Rs 565bn fresh financing.
historical_instances.source: Arab News / Pakistan Banks Association reporting, late 2025

cases.playbook: Within 2 trading days: BUY HUBC and KEL on the Power IPP cash-unlock. Within 5 trading days: ADD PSO (largest single creditor). E&P names (OGDC/PPL/Mari) lag by 1-2 weeks then catch +3-5%. Banks (UBL/MCB/HBL) lag by 2-4 weeks for a smaller +2-4% as legacy power exposure de-risks. Hold 30-45 days; trim into the run-up to the next quarterly results. **The case fires only during the first 7 days post-announcement — after that the trader's holding the position, the system isn't re-firing.**
cases.what_breaks_it: If the resolution is announced but financing structure is unclear, or if SBP signals a pushback on the NIM concession to banks. Then the rally fades within 5 days.
cases.research_basis: Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx — Power Sector / Circular Debt section. Tier-0 patch evaluation (data/_health/backtest_24m_2026-05-03.md): the case's miss rate jumped from ~32% expected to 76% in the 24-month replay because the 60-day event decay caused 27 re-firings of a 5-day catalyst. Shrinking the event decay to 7 days keeps the original 1-event/window fire pattern and recovers ~12% of total Layer-1 misses.

reactions.narrative_note: Circular-debt clearance unlocks Power/OMC/E&P/Banks — bias up.

cases.title: Power-sector circular debt build-up >= Rs 200bn / quarter
cases.pattern: Quarterly circular-debt build-up accelerates without offsetting government action. IPP receivables stretch, PSO inventory days balloon, banks provision against power exposure.

historical_instances.context: Cumulative circular debt crossed Rs 2.4 trillion before the Dec-2025 settlement. Quarterly build-up of Rs 230-280bn in mid-2024.

cases.playbook: TRIM HUBC/KEL/PSO if held in size; AVOID fresh BUY on Power names until government tabular plan is published. Banks with disclosed power exposure (UBL, HBL) see -1 to -3% sympathy moves over 21 days; not exit-worthy on its own but worth a HOLD downgrade if value lens is also weak.
cases.what_breaks_it: If a credible resolution plan is announced within ~30 days, the move reverses sharply (see circular_debt_resolution_large above).
cases.research_basis: Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx — Power Sector / Circular Debt section.

reactions.narrative_note: Power-sector circular debt worsening — TRIM IPPs and PSO.

cases.title: FIRST SBP rate cut of a cycle (within 5 days of decision)
cases.pattern: SBP delivers the very first rate cut of a new easing cycle. Historically the strongest single bullish catalyst on PSX — leveraged sectors (Cement, Conglomerate/Chem, OMC) re-rate first; Power, Real Estate, Auto follow. Distinct from later cuts in the cycle which often see profit-taking (see nth_rate_cut_profit_taking).

historical_instances.context: First cut of the cycle: 22% -> 20.5% (-150bp). CPI had eased from 38% peak to 11.8% YoY.

cases.playbook: BUY/ADD Cement (DGKC, MLCF, LUCK, FCCL) and Conglomerate/Chem (ENGRO, LOTCHEM) within 2 trading days. EXPECT 8-15% upside in the leveraged names within 30-45 days. Banks underperform by 2-4% over the same window (NIM compression). DO NOT chase if the cut comes with a hawkish forward-guidance statement — the move halves.
cases.what_breaks_it: Hawkish SBP guidance, IMF resistance, or a PKR shock within 5 days of the decision.
cases.research_basis: Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx — Macroeconomic Determinants (SBP Policy Rate); Mubarik (2002), Husain & Mahmood (1999) findings on monetary-policy transmission.

reactions.narrative_note: First rate cut of cycle — Cement / Conglomerate / Auto leg up; Banks NIM compresses.

cases.title: SBP rate hike SHOCK (>=100bps, within 5 days of decision)
cases.pattern: SBP delivers a SHOCK rate hike of >=100bps. Historically pressures every leveraged name (Cement, Auto, Conglomerate) and broad market — but ONLY in the first 5-7 trading days; the move is usually fully priced in within 2 weeks. Freshness gate prevents the case from firing on a stale 3-week-old hike that has already been digested. **Tier-0 patch (2026-05-03):** added `last_hike_bps_gte:100` so the case ignores routine 25-50bp adjustments; only major shocks (the 2022 emergency hikes, the 2023-06 100bp hike) qualify. The 25bp threshold was firing on adjustments that produced no defensive follow-through and dragged precision.

historical_instances.context: Emergency 100bp hike to 22%. Pre-IMF-deal stress; PKR under pressure.

cases.playbook: Lean DEFENSIVE for 5-10 trading days. TRIM Cement/Auto/Conglomerate exposure. Banking is mixed: NIM expansion is bullish for HBL/UBL/MCB on day-1 (+1-2%), but credit-cost concerns drag them flat over 21 days. E&Ps (OGDC/PPL) outperform by ~3% on PKR weakness. **Routine 25-50bp adjustments do NOT trip this case** — only shocks (>=100bps) historically produced the defensive signature.
cases.what_breaks_it: If the hike is paired with credible IMF news, the move reverses within 10 days. AFTER 5 trading days the move is typically fully priced in — do not stay defensive on a hike that's already 1-2 weeks old. **Hard guard**: the case requires >=100bps; smaller hikes are silent (the 2024 25-50bp adjustments produced no defensive follow-through in the replay).
cases.research_basis: Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx — Macroeconomic Determinants. Historical replay shows the rate-up driver in macro_impact stays active for 21 days after a hike, but the directional drag is reliable only in the first ~5 trading days AND only on >=100bps shocks (2022-Jun 150bp, 2023-Jun 100bp emergency hike). Routine 25-50bp tweaks did not produce the same signature. Tier-0 patch evaluation (data/_health/backtest_24m_2026-05-03.md) added the magnitude gate.

reactions.narrative_note: SBP rate-hike shock — defensive 5-10d. TRIM Cement/Auto/Conglomerate.

cases.title: Rate-cycle pivot — first cut OR first hike of a new cycle
cases.pattern: Within 5 trading days of a rate decision that flips the cycle direction (a CUT after a hiking cycle, or a HIKE after a cutting cycle). Surfaces the regime change for the strategist's qualitative re-evaluation; does NOT prescribe a mechanical action because the post-pivot reaction depends entirely on (a) the size of the surprise vs. consensus, (b) the IMF / FX backdrop, and (c) the size of the run-up before the pivot. The historical record is too small to mechanise (n=2 since 2020 at this granularity), so this case exists as a **regime-change flag** rather than a buy/sell rule.

historical_instances.context: First cut of the post-2022 cycle: 22% -> 20.5% (-150bp). CPI had eased from 38% peak to 11.8% YoY.
Final hike of the tightening cycle: 21% -> 22% (+100bp). PKR under pressure, pre-IMF deal stress.

cases.playbook: DIAGNOSTIC ONLY — re-evaluate everything qualitatively. The Master Strategist should: (a) name the pivot in the narrative, (b) explicitly check whether the size of the move beat / missed consensus, (c) check whether the IMF backdrop and PKR are stable, (d) look at the universe 5-day return BEFORE the pivot — a stretched 5%+ pre-pivot run-up means the cut/hike is largely priced in and the move can fade in 5-10 trading days. Do NOT mechanically TRIM or ADD on this signal alone; treat it as a regime flag that requires fresh first-principles reasoning.
cases.what_breaks_it: Once the cycle has 2+ moves in the new direction, this case stops firing (rate_cuts_180d_eq:1 is exclusive). At that point the regime is established and other cases (sbp_rate_cut_cycle_initiation, post_cut_cycle_continuation) take over.
cases.research_basis: Validation harness 2026-05-02 (scripts/validate_strategy_fixes.py): the previous mechanical 'Nth cut profit-taking' rule had 0/10 hit rate and predicted the wrong direction by +6.2pp. Replaced with a qualitative-flag-only case. Historical record (n=2 since 2020) is too thin for a mechanical action, but the regime change is real and worth surfacing for Claude's reasoning.

reactions.narrative_note: Rate-cycle pivot detected — surface explicitly in narrative; no auto-action.

cases.title: Post-cut continuation rally (cycle bottom + 14-60 days)
cases.pattern: Once SBP has delivered >=4 cuts and the rate has stabilised for 2+ weeks, PSX historically continues to grind higher (+5-12% over 21 days) as the rate-cut transmission flows into corporate earnings and banking system liquidity rebuilds. The rally is broader than the initial first-cut spike: small-mid caps and value names that lagged the leveraged-sector pop now catch up.

historical_instances.context: Seven cuts delivered (22% -> 11%); last cut 5-May-2025. Market drifted higher as transmission flowed into Q4-FY25 earnings.
Eight cuts (22% -> 11%); last cut 5-May-2025 (4 months prior, slightly outside the 60d window in this instance but pattern holds).

cases.playbook: BIAS LONG. Add small/mid-cap value (PABC, EPCL, NPL, KOHC) and value-with-quality names that lagged the leveraged-sector first-leg rally. Banks regain favour as loan growth recovers. Hold 30-45 days; the trade fades when the next hike or major macro shock arrives. AVOID chasing the leveraged-sector names that already ran in the first-cut window — they are reaching the high end of their fair-value bands.
cases.what_breaks_it: A surprise RATE HIKE (e.g. circular-debt wash-up cost, see 2025-12-15) or a fresh PKR-shock breaks the rally cleanly. Also broken if FIPI flips to sustained net selling.
cases.research_basis: Historical replay of 2025-06-06 and 2025-09-08 windows. Consistent with the SBP transmission-lag literature: corporate financial-cost relief takes 1-2 quarters to show in EPS, by which point banking-system liquidity has also rebuilt and PSX broadens.

reactions.narrative_note: Post-cut continuation — small/mid value catches up.

cases.title: IMF Stand-By Arrangement / EFF approval — ONLY when not already priced in (>10% pre-rally)
cases.pattern: IMF Executive Board approves a new SBA or EFF for Pakistan. Removes default tail-risk, FX reserves rebuild signalled, and CDS spreads compress sharply. **Tier-1 patch (2026-05-03):** added a `kse100_21d_lte:0.10` rally-already-priced suppressor so the case ignores approvals that arrive after the market has already rallied >10% in 21 days. Validation against the two historical instances showed 2023-07-12 had +7.6% pre-rally (still under threshold, case fires) and 2024-09-25 had -0.3% (well under threshold, case fires) — both 100% hits preserved. The 10% guard is twice as wide as the imf_review suppressor (5%) because IMF approvals are an order of magnitude bigger catalysts and can absorb a larger pre-rally without the trade going stale.

historical_instances.context: $3bn 9-month SBA approved.
$7bn 37-month EFF approved.

cases.playbook: Day-of and day-after: ADD large-cap Banks (HBL, UBL, MCB) and E&Ps (OGDC, PPL). Expect KSE-100 +5-8% in 5d, +8-12% in 21d. Banks are the cleanest beat (sovereign-spread compression directly improves treasury MTM gains). **If KSE-100 has already rallied >10% in the prior 21 days, the case will not fire** — re-evaluate qualitatively because the rally has already discounted most of the IMF impact.
cases.what_breaks_it: If the approval comes with severe conditionality leaks (e.g. major tariff hikes, new revenue measures), the move halves. **Hard guard**: trailing 21d KSE-100 return >10% suppresses the case (the news is already in the price; further upside is below 5% on average from such elevated entry points).
cases.research_basis: Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx — IMF / Sovereign Risk section. Tier-1 patch evaluation (2026-05-03): the 24-month replay showed both historical SBA/EFF approvals had pre-rally <10% (2023-07-12: +7.6%; 2024-09-25: -0.3%), so the 10% guard preserves the case's 100% hit rate while protecting against a hypothetical 'announcement after a +12-15% pre-rally' scenario where reward / risk inverts.

reactions.narrative_note: IMF program approved — E&P / Cement / OMC relief lift; Power -1 notch (tariff-hike pressure on collections, verified 2024-09/10 -11% to -14% Power moves).

cases.title: IMF program review completed (SLA or board approval, tranche released) — ONLY when not already priced in
cases.pattern: Distinct from the initial SBA/EFF approval: IMF reviews close every ~3 months and trigger a tranche disbursement (typically $700m-$1.1bn). End-to-end backtest revealed the **21-day reaction is noisy and direction-mixed** (the early bullish print fades into routine quarterly chop), but the **5-day reaction is reliably positive** when the macro backdrop is benign (no concurrent rate-up / pkr_weak shock). Treat as a 5-day tactical window with MIXED 21-day expectations, not a 21-day directional bet. **Tier-0 patches (2026-05-03):** (1) the underlying `imf_review_completed` event decay was shortened from 21 -> 5 days so the case fires only during the actually-positive 5-day window — the 21-day decay was firing the case 18 times per 24-month replay with a 5.6% hit rate as the 'early print fades' problem caught up with us. (2) Added a `kse100_21d_lte:0.05` rally-already-priced suppressor as a belt-and-braces guard for the live system: block the BUY when the trailing 21d return is already above 5%.

historical_instances.context: First SBA review staff-level agreement (formal board approval Nov-15). KSE-100 +3.8% in 5d, +12.1% in 21d (the long-tail rally was driven by IMF + benign macro confluence, not the review itself).
historical_instances.source: IMF press release, October 2023; PSX EOD.

historical_instances.context: SBA second review and final tranche ($1.1bn). Tighter d5/d21 signature: +2.8% / +5.8%.
First EFF review SLA. Backtest shows d5 was modestly positive but d21 turned negative as the market re-focused on PKR + circular-debt overhang. Confirms the 'd5 wins, d21 noisy' framing.

cases.playbook: Day-of and day-1: ADD large-cap Banks (HBL, UBL, MCB) and E&Ps (OGDC, PPL) for a **5-day tactical window**; magnitude ~30-50% of the initial SBA/EFF approval reaction. **Do not extend the trade past 10 trading days** — the 21-day signature is mixed and fades into whatever the next macro driver is. AVOID chasing into the close of day-1; better entry on day-2 morning weakness. **If KSE-100 has already rallied >5% in the prior 21 days, the case will not fire** (rally-already-priced suppressor) — re-evaluate qualitatively rather than blindly buy the news.
cases.what_breaks_it: If the review SLA carries delayed conditionality leaks or a missed structural benchmark, the rally fades within 5 days. Also broken by a concurrent PKR or rate shock — 21-day mean turns negative when those drivers are also active. **Hard guard**: trailing 21d return >5% suppresses the case entirely (the news is already in the price).
cases.research_basis: Historical replay of 2023-10, 2024-04, 2025-03, 2025-10 reviews PLUS end-to-end test 2026-05-02 (data/_health/end_to_end_test.md): d5 reliably positive; d21 mixed (-5.7% mean when other macro drivers are concurrent). Strategy-fixes evaluation 2026-05-02 prescribed the d5 flip + MIXED bias framing. Tier-0 patch evaluation (data/_health/backtest_24m_2026-05-03.md): 17 of 53 misses traced to firings where KSE-100 trailing 21d was already >5% — the rally-already-priced suppressor recovers ~32% of the case's miss rate without sacrificing valid hits.

reactions.narrative_note: IMF review completed — Banking-only relief lift. E&P dropped (backtest 0% accuracy).

cases.title: PKR weakens >= 5% vs USD in one trading week
cases.pattern: USD/PKR moves sharply (>=5%) over 5 trading days. Pre-2023 typically followed by a market-wide sell-off; post-IMF-2024 the reaction is sector-specific (E&Ps and textile exporters benefit; Cement/Auto/Pharma hurt).

historical_instances.context: Cap removed from interbank rate; PKR fell ~10% in 3 days.

cases.playbook: Day-1: ADD E&P names (OGDC, PPL, MARI) on the wellhead-USD-link tailwind. TRIM Cement (imported coal cost shock), Auto (CKD parts), Pharma (API imports). Watch for follow-on policy-rate hike within 2 weeks (raises probability of CAUTION regime).
cases.what_breaks_it: If the move is reversed by SBP intervention within 3 days, all positions revert.
cases.research_basis: Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx — Exchange Rate / Sectoral Sensitivities.

reactions.narrative_note: PKR STRONG devaluation — E&P wellhead-USD tailwind; Autos pressured by imports. Cement downgrade DROPPED (again, after STRONG-only filter still scored 38% — Cement's PKR response is non-stationary).

cases.title: FIPI net outflow >= Rs 1.0bn over 5 trading days
cases.pattern: Foreign investors dump >Rs 1.0bn net over 5 trading days. PSX is local-flow-driven, so foreign capitulation is a contrarian signal IF it isn't accompanied by macro deterioration. If it IS accompanied by macro deterioration (rate-up + PKR-weak + CAUTION regime), it's a true risk-off and tends to extend. **Tier-1 patch (2026-05-03):** threshold loosened from -Rs 1.5bn to -Rs 1.0bn so the case fires on the more common Rs 1-1.5bn outflow week (which already meets the contrarian-buy criterion) — the original 1.5bn bar was too high for routine flow regimes. The case is orphan in the historical 24-month replay because the SCStrade FIPI parquet only carries 5 days of live data; backfilling FIPI history is queued for a future sprint.

historical_instances.context: Net foreign outflow ~Rs 2.1bn / 5d, no concurrent macro shock.
Net outflow ~Rs 1.8bn / 5d AND rate at 21% AND IMF deal stalled.

cases.playbook: FIRST check the macro_lens. If macro is benign (NORMAL regime, no PKR/rate shock), this is a contrarian BUY signal — large-cap value names (HBL, UBL, OGDC) historically recover within 21 days. If macro is hostile (CAUTION/CRISIS or active driver:rate_up / driver:pkr_weak), TRIM and shift to CAUTIOUS stance — the outflow tends to extend.
cases.what_breaks_it: Confluence with macro stress flips the sign of the trade.
cases.research_basis: Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx — Behavioural Mechanics / Foreign Flows; PSX is local-flow dominated.

reactions.narrative_note: FIPI capitulation — contrarian setup IF macro is benign (check NORMAL regime).

cases.title: Universe down >= 5% in 5 days AND breadth < 30% AND sentiment <= -0.4
cases.pattern: Three-way confluence: realised universe loss, narrow breadth, and bearish-tilted news sentiment. Classic herding capitulation on PSX — the kind the research doc identifies as the recurring 'emotional' risk-off pattern. Tends to mean-revert within 5-10 trading days IF macro is benign.

historical_instances.context: Election overhang + sympathy with Indian VIX spike. No macro shock.

cases.playbook: If macro_lens reads benign: this is a tactical BUY window. The fastest recovery names are the high-quality blue-chips (HUBC, OGDC, MEBL, UBL) — large foreign names that get sold in herd panics and bid back by locals once the tape stabilises. Wait for one green-close confirmation day before adding (avoids catching a falling knife). Hold 5-10 trading days, then re-evaluate against Phase-1.
cases.what_breaks_it: If macro IS hostile (active rate-up / pkr_weak / circular_debt_worsening), the panic extends — DEFENSIVE stance is correct.
cases.research_basis: Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx — Behavioural Mechanics (Herding, Overreaction).

reactions.narrative_note: 3-day panic — tactical BUY in blue-chips IF macro benign.

cases.title: Phase-1 in CASH while breadth and FIPI are improving
cases.pattern: Phase-1 mechanical rule says CASH (universe 150d momentum negative) BUT breadth has clawed above 50% and FIPI has flipped net positive over 5 days. This is the classic regime-change blind spot of trend-following rules — the rule reacts to the long lookback while the live tape has already turned.

historical_instances.context: 150d momentum still negative from H1-2023 drawdown, but Aug FIPI inflows + IMF tranche disbursed.

cases.playbook: DO NOT mechanically dismiss the Phase-1 CASH call — the rule earns its keep over decades of data. But this is one of the few legitimate occasions to flag a HALF-WEIGHT POSITIONING decision (e.g. take 50% of a normal position in 1-2 large-cap value names). The Master Strategist must explicitly set agrees_with_phase1=false and write the override note.
cases.what_breaks_it: If FIPI flips back negative or breadth re-narrows within 10 days, exit and revert to CASH.
cases.research_basis: Trend-following blind spots are well documented in momentum literature (Antonacci, Asness). This case adapts the principle to PSX-specific flow indicators.

reactions.narrative_note: Phase-1 CASH while breadth/FIPI improving — keep dry powder, watch for confirmation.

cases.title: Multiple universe names in 5d earnings blackout
cases.pattern: >=2 universe names have earnings within 5 trading days. Earnings days on PSX routinely produce 5-10% gaps; a concentration of blackouts means a single bad print can take a meaningful percent of the book down in one day.
cases.playbook: Recommend HOLD-only on the blackout names (no fresh BUY/ADD), and downgrade conviction by one bucket on the strategist's overall stance. If 3+ names are in blackout, set risk_stance to CAUTIOUS regardless of macro tape — the idiosyncratic earnings risk dominates the macro signal for the next week.
cases.what_breaks_it: Doesn't break — this is a structural risk-management overlay, not a directional view.
cases.research_basis: Standard event-risk management. Earnings-day gap distributions on PSX have ~3-4x the daily vol of non-event days.

reactions.narrative_note: Multiple universe names in 5d earnings blackout — HOLD-only, downgrade conviction.

cases.title: Imported coal price spikes >= 15% in 30 days
cases.pattern: Coal price spikes hit Cement margins directly (50-60% of variable cost). Effect is largest on north-zone players (DGKC, MLCF) due to higher coal-mix vs FFC/LUCK who blend more local Thar coal.

historical_instances.context: Russia-Ukraine; coal +52% / 30d.

cases.playbook: TRIM north-zone Cement (DGKC, MLCF) over 5-10 trading days. LUCK and FCCL are relatively defensive but still expect -3 to -5% over 21d. AVOID fresh BUY in the sector until coal stabilises for >=5 sessions.
cases.what_breaks_it: Government cement-price hike approval within 30 days reverses the move (rare but happens).
cases.research_basis: Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx — Sectoral Sensitivities (Cement).

reactions.narrative_note: Imported coal shock — TRIM north-zone Cement (DGKC, MLCF).

cases.title: Brent up >= 10% in 21 days
cases.pattern: Brent spike directly lifts E&P USD-linked wellhead pricing, with PKR-weakness amplifier. OMC margins are formula-protected so they're roughly neutral; Cement (transport cost) and Auto (commodity) see a small drag.

historical_instances.context: Brent +28% / 21d on Russia-Ukraine.

cases.playbook: ADD E&P (OGDC, PPL, MARI) on day-1 if not already overweight. Expect +10-18% in 21 days. PSO is roughly neutral after the formula pass-through. AVOID adding Cement / Auto into a strong oil tape.
cases.what_breaks_it: If Brent reverses sharply within 5 days, the trade fades.
cases.research_basis: Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx — Sectoral Sensitivities (E&P).

reactions.narrative_note: Brent up AND market healthy (breadth >40% AND universe 5d > -3%) — clean E&P upgrade trade. (Round-2 added the breadth/regime guards: prior case fired E&P upgrade in oil-spike-as-risk-off regimes and was wrong-direction —49% accuracy.)

cases.title: Within 14 days of a general election (federal or provincial)
cases.pattern: Pre-election windows on PSX historically produce wider intra-day ranges, lower foreign participation, and a flat-to-down market on average. Volatility comes from headline risk; directional moves only emerge after results clarity.

historical_instances.context: General election, contested results.

cases.playbook: Set CAUTIOUS stance for the 7-10 trading days before the event. AVOID fresh BUY/ADD on illiquid mid-caps (wider spreads). HOLD existing book; don't TRIM unless something else is broken — the pre-election dip historically mean-reverts within 21 days post-result.
cases.what_breaks_it: A clean, decisive result with credible coalition signal can produce a relief rally within 2-3 sessions.
cases.research_basis: Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx — Political Risk / Behavioural Mechanics.

reactions.narrative_note: Election window — half-size all new entries; AVOID illiquid mid-caps.

cases.title: Mutual fund accumulation: 3+ month streak with broad fund participation
cases.pattern: At least one universe stock has shown net positive flow from PSX equity mutual funds for 3+ consecutive months, with 5+ funds raising their position in the latest reporting month. Smart money has been quietly building -- retail typically chases within 30-60 days.

historical_instances.context: Jun-25 AHL MF Holdings: 14 new entrants in top 10 holdings vs May-25 (NRL, FCEPL, FLYNG, NATF, TPLT, BFAGRO and others). Equity AUMs grew from 10.7% to 12% of industry MoM.
historical_instances.source: AHL Mutual Funds Equity Holdings, Jul-17-2025.

cases.playbook: BUY/ADD the names showing the 3-month streak with broad fund participation -- they are the highest-conviction institutional adds on the tape. 21-day expected return is +5-9%, primarily from retail catch-up after AHL's monthly report publication. Hold 30-45 days; trim into a -1.5x ATR pullback. Largest signal at month-end (post AHL publish).
cases.what_breaks_it: If FIPI flips strongly negative (foreign sells overwhelm local fund buying) OR if the latest AHL report shows a sharp deceleration of the streak (n_funds_increasing drops below 3).
cases.research_basis: Khan & Mahmood (2018) -- institutional flows on PSX lead retail by ~30-45 days. AHL Research monthly Mutual Funds Equity Holdings reports (2024-2026 series).

reactions.narrative_note: MF accumulation broad streak — names auto-listed as institutional-buy candidates.

cases.title: Mutual fund distribution: 3+ month outflow streak
cases.pattern: At least one universe stock has experienced net selling from PSX equity mutual funds for 3+ consecutive months. Even without a fundamental catalyst, sustained institutional distribution drains liquidity and caps any rally; reversals require a clean macro shock or earnings beat.
cases.playbook: TRIM or HOLD-only on names with 3+ months of distribution. AVOID fresh BUY even if technicals look constructive -- institutional selling has historically capped 5-7% rallies on the way down. Consider exiting full position if a 4th consecutive month of distribution prints.
cases.what_breaks_it: A genuine sector-wide rerating catalyst (e.g. circular-debt resolution for Power, oil shock for E&P) overrides the flow signal.
cases.research_basis: Same series as mf_accumulation_strong; the bearish corollary.

reactions.narrative_note: MF distribution streak — TRIM/HOLD-only on flagged names.

cases.title: 3+ funds open a NEW position in a universe stock in one month
cases.pattern: Three or more mutual funds open a brand-new position (zero -> non-zero) in the same stock in a single reporting month. This is a much higher-conviction signal than incremental adds because it requires NEW funds making a fresh thesis bet -- typically follows an earnings beat, restructuring, or sector tailwind that the funds are jointly identifying.
cases.playbook: BUY (full size) within 5 trading days of AHL report publication. Initiation clusters historically deliver +8-14% in 60 days vs +2-3% for ordinary fund adds (Pakistani studies on institutional flows). Hold 60-90 days; first profit-take target is +10%, second is +18%.
cases.what_breaks_it: If the next month's AHL report shows the funds reduce or exit their fresh position, treat as failed thesis and exit.
cases.research_basis: AHL Mutual Funds Equity Holdings reports (Jun-25 specifically called out 14 new entrants vs May-25). Khan & Mahmood (2018).

reactions.narrative_note: 3+ funds initiated — BUY full size on flagged names within 5 sessions.

cases.title: Mutual fund distribution + name in BUY_VALUE band
cases.pattern: Mutual funds have been distributing a stock for 2+ months WHILE the value-book classifies multiple universe names as BUY_VALUE. Classic capitulation pattern -- forced selling near a value-attractive level. Reversals from this configuration historically generate +15-25% over 60 days when the macro tape stabilises. **Tier-1 patch (2026-05-03):** triggers loosened (distribution streak 3->2 months, n_buy_value 3->2 names) so the case fires on the more common 2-month distribution + 2-name value-band pattern that still satisfies the capitulation thesis. The case is orphan in the historical 24-month replay because the value-book is not populated by the historical-replay path (per-stock PE/PB/quality scores are computed against current fundamentals, not as-of-date); the live system has full value-book coverage.
cases.playbook: Contrarian BUY in 1/3-position increments on the names that overlap (distribution AND BUY_VALUE). Wait for 1-2 sessions of stabilisation (no new lows) before adding. Hold 60-90 days; expect +12-22% on the recovery. Stop is the 21-day low minus 1 ATR.
cases.what_breaks_it: If macro_impact emits a STRONG negative driver in the same window (e.g. fx_blowout, rate_up STRONG), the trade fails -- distribution accelerates from a different cause.
cases.research_basis: Standard mean-reversion + value-trap research; needs at least 2 PSX historical instances before confidence promotion to MEDIUM. Add to the case as they're observed.

reactions.narrative_note: MF distribution + BUY_VALUE overlap — contrarian 1/3-position increments.

cases.title: Mutual funds accumulating while price is flat / lagging
cases.pattern: Mutual funds adding meaningfully (180-day flow >= +2% of free float) while the broad market is flat or down (universe 5-day return <= 1%). Information asymmetry signal -- funds typically have early visibility on operational improvements, government tenders, and dividend changes 1-2 quarters before they show in price.
cases.playbook: BUY/ADD the divergent names within 5 trading days. The setup typically resolves to the upside within 30-90 days as the catalyst the funds anticipated becomes public. Position sizing 50-75% of normal because the catalyst is unidentified -- size up if a positive catalyst materialises.
cases.what_breaks_it: If the broad market rolls over hard (universe_5d < -3%), even smart-money names get sold; revisit after 5 sessions.
cases.research_basis: Information asymmetry literature on emerging markets (Bekaert & Harvey, 1998 -- adapted to PSX context). The 6-month accumulation / flat-price divergence is the user-requested signal from the Apr-2026 sprint.

reactions.narrative_note: MF accumulation while price flat — institutional setup, BUY/ADD divergent names.

cases.title: Policy rate in TOP-quartile regime — bank NIM tailwind
cases.pattern: When the SBP policy rate sits in the top quartile of its trailing 5-year range, banks structurally earn fat spreads on their large T-bill / PIB book against largely-fixed CASA (current-account / savings-account) deposit costs. Validation harness measured the bank basket (FABL, MCB, MEBL, ABL, BAHL, HBL, NBP, UBL) returned +23.55% over the next 90 days during top-quartile months versus +9.91% during bottom-quartile months — a 13.6pp edge — across 6 years of PSX history. The signal is a **regime tailwind**, not a single-day catalyst, so size and horizon should reflect that.

historical_instances.context: Policy rate at 22% (top of 5y range); bank basket subsequently rallied through Q3-Q4 2023 as treasury MTM gains accumulated and CASA cost lagged the rate move.
historical_instances.source: PSX EOD; SBP policy rate history; strategy-fixes validation 2026-05-02.

cases.playbook: BIAS LONG large-cap banks for a 60-90 day horizon. Preferred names: HBL, UBL, MCB (largest treasury books, most CASA-heavy). Position sizing can be 100% of normal because the historical edge (13.6pp / 90d) materially compensates the cost of carry. Do NOT chase if PKR is simultaneously weakening sharply (offsetting credit-quality concern) — wait for one stable PKR week. Trim into the first SBP rate cut, which historically marks the top of the bank-trade.
cases.what_breaks_it: (a) PKR shock >5% in a week (asset-quality concerns swamp NIM benefit). (b) The first rate cut of a new easing cycle — bank trade tops within 5-10 trading days as the market prices forward NIM compression. (c) A new circular-debt worsening event (banks re-provision power-sector exposure).
cases.research_basis: scripts/validate_strategy_fixes.py TEST T3b (2026-05-02): policy_rate top-quartile (>=20.5% in our 6y window) vs bottom-quartile (<=8.5%) → bank basket +23.55% vs +9.91% over 90 days. n=20 high-rate months vs n=20 low-rate months. Threshold lowered to 18% to fire earlier in the regime (top decile is 20.5%; the lift is monotone above ~16%).

reactions.narrative_note: Policy rate top-quartile — large-cap Bank NIM tailwind, BIAS LONG 60-90d.

cases.title: Policy rate in BOTTOM-quartile regime — bank NIM headwind
cases.pattern: Symmetric companion to banking_nim_regime_high. When the SBP policy rate sits in the bottom quartile of its trailing 5-year range, bank treasury yields compress while CASA cost stays sticky on the way down — net interest income contracts. The validation harness measured bank-basket returns of +9.91% over 90 days during bottom-quartile months, only modestly positive in nominal terms and meaningfully UNDER-performing the broad universe (which benefits from the rate cut via leveraged sectors).

historical_instances.context: Policy rate at 7% (COVID-cycle low); bank basket lagged the broad market by ~10pp over the next 90 days as the broad market rallied on rate-cut transmission to leveraged sectors.

cases.playbook: BIAS NEUTRAL or UNDERWEIGHT large-cap banks. The trade is not necessarily SHORT — banks may still grind +9% in nominal terms over 90 days — but the **opportunity cost is high** because leveraged sectors (Cement, OMC, Conglomerate) historically deliver +15-25% in the same window. Rotate the bank weight into Cement / Conglomerate / Power instead. Preferred substitutes: DGKC, MLCF, FCCL, ENGRO, HUBC.
cases.what_breaks_it: (a) A surprise rate hike (banks re-rate sharply in 5 days). (b) A circular-debt resolution event (banks lift on legacy-loan de-risking). (c) An IMF SBA / EFF approval (banks lead the rally on sovereign-spread compression).
cases.research_basis: scripts/validate_strategy_fixes.py TEST T3b (2026-05-02): symmetric companion to banking_nim_regime_high. Bank basket +9.91% / 90d in bottom-quartile policy-rate regime vs +23.55% / 90d in top-quartile regime. Threshold raised to 9% to capture early entries into the low-rate regime; bottom decile is 8.5%.

reactions.narrative_note: Policy rate bottom-quartile — Bank NIM headwind narrative. Auto-downgrade REMOVED 2026-05-13 (backtest: banks actually outperformed +0.62% vs universe in 196 low-rate sessions; rule was wrong direction).

cases.title: Broad mutual-fund distribution across the top-30 universe
cases.pattern: The latest AHL Mutual Funds Equity Holdings summary table flags 5+ top-30 names with negative MoM change (% of equity AUMs). Industry-wide rebalancing -- usually a defensive rotation out of equities into cash / debt -- and a forward-21d headwind for the broad index. Uses the AHL summary parquet's own MoM column so it works on summary-only PDFs (i.e. when per-fund detail is unavailable).

historical_instances.context: Jan-26 AHL report: 21 of top-30 names print negative MoM (PSO -0.9pp, FFC -0.8pp, LUCK -0.6pp, MEBL -0.6pp, HUBC -0.5pp, NBP -0.5pp, SNGP -0.4pp, KOHC -0.3pp, BAHL -0.2pp, BAFL -0.2pp, OGDC -0.2pp, HMB -0.1pp). Total top-30 holdings down -4.4pp MoM.
historical_instances.source: AHL Mutual Funds Equity Holdings, Feb-18-2026 publication.

cases.playbook: Reduce gross exposure 10-20% across the broad book (especially top-30 names with the worst MoM print). Avoid fresh BUY in the worst-distributed sectors until next month's report shows stabilisation (n_distributed_30d <= 3). The signal is universe-level, not name-level -- use it as a regime modifier on top of macro / valuation work, not a rifle-shot.
cases.what_breaks_it: If FIPI flips strongly positive (foreign inflows offset local fund selling) OR if the next AHL report shows the top-30 distribution count dropping below 3.
cases.research_basis: AHL Mutual Funds Equity Holdings Jan-26 report, plus general flow-of-funds literature on local-mutual-fund-led drawdowns in frontier markets.

reactions.narrative_note: Broad MF distribution across top-30 universe — reduce gross exposure 10-20%.

cases.title: BROAD volume-confirmed breakouts (>=5 universe names in last 3 sessions)
cases.pattern: At least 5 universe stocks have printed a confirmed breakout in the last 3 trading sessions: a same-day move of >=2% on >=2x the trailing-20-day median volume. Tier-1 patch (2026-05-03) tightened the threshold from `count_gte:1` to `count_gte:5` after the first 24m backtest revealed `gte:1` fired on 306/313 dates (97%) — too permissive to be informative. The 5-name threshold (~14% of the 36-name universe) requires BROAD participation: any single name can breakout on idiosyncratic news, but five names breaking out together signals universe momentum. Validation harness measured (2026-05-02, scripts/validate_strategy_fixes.py TEST T4): a +1.5% day on >=1.5x median volume returned +0.80% over the next 5 trading days on average across n=4,657 PSX observations, versus +0.23% on +1.5% days with <=0.7x median volume (n=734) -- a +0.57pp 5-day edge for high-volume confirmation. We tighten the live thresholds to 2.0% / 2.0x to reduce false positives. The signal is tactical, not strategic -- 5-day horizon, modest size.

historical_instances.context: Validation harness summary: across 5y of 36-stock PSX OHLCV, 4,657 high-volume +1.5% days returned +0.80% over 5d vs +0.23% on 734 low-volume +1.5% days. The historical sample is statistically robust (n>>30 in both buckets); per-name instances are too numerous to enumerate here.

cases.playbook: Surface the breakout name(s) explicitly in the narrative. Lean direction (BUY/HOLD bias) on the named stock for a 5-day tactical window with HALF-NORMAL position sizing -- the +0.57pp 5d edge is meaningful but tactical, not strategic. Combine with the per-stock value/quality lens before sizing up: a volume-confirmed breakout in a HIGH-quality / BUY_VALUE name deserves more weight than the same signal on a JUNK name (where it likely reflects a short-squeeze rather than informed buying). Profit-take target: +3% from breakout-day close, or 5 trading days, whichever comes first.
cases.what_breaks_it: (a) The breakout day was driven by a corporate action (announced split, dividend XD, rights issue) -- check the news lens before sizing. (b) Universe is in CRISIS regime -- correlation collapse means even confirmed breakouts get sold. (c) The stock has a known earnings event within 3 trading days (the breakout is positioning for the print, and the gap-risk after the print swamps the volume edge).
cases.research_basis: Validation harness 2026-05-02 (scripts/validate_strategy_fixes.py TEST T4): n=4,657 high-volume up days vs n=734 low-volume up days across 36-stock PSX universe over 5 years. Forward-5d return edge = +0.57pp. Confirms that the generic 'volume confirms direction' rule DOES hold on PSX despite the market being retail-heavy.

reactions.narrative_note: Broad volume-confirmed breakouts — 5d tactical signal. Position-size haircut REMOVED 2026-05-13 (backtest +0.32% edge per fire; haircut was neutralising the alpha).

cases.title: US-Iran tension oil spike (Hormuz risk premium ON)
cases.pattern: US-Iran military tension flares (strikes, ceasefire breakdown, Strait of Hormuz threats). Brent jumps 5-15% in a week then partially mean-reverts as ceasefire/de-escalation news lands. PSX experiences global risk-off in week 1 (KSE-100 -1 to -2%) followed by a sector rotation: E&P (OGDC/PPL/MARI/POL) benefit from the oil floor, OMC/refining (PSO/APL/ATRL) get squeezed by margin compression and inventory mark-to-market, banks track global EM risk-off (-1 to -2%), cement is neutral-to-positive (lower freight if Brent fades). The pattern repeats: 2024-04 (drone strikes), 2024-10 (Israel-Iran direct exchange), 2026-05 (current — Strait of Hormuz reopening dispute).

historical_instances.context: Iran direct drone/missile strike on Israel. Brent spiked from $87 to $92. KSE-100 -1.4% next session, then sector rotation: E&P up, OMC down.
Iran fires 180+ ballistic missiles at Israel. Brent +5% in 3 days. EM equities -1.5%, PSX -1.2% next session.

cases.playbook: BUY/ADD E&P (OGDC, PPL, MARI, POL) on the dip — the Brent floor + Pakistan-specific royalty/realisation lift produces +3-7% over 5-21 days. AVOID/TRIM OMC and refining (PSO, APL, ATRL) — margin compression + inventory MTM losses. HOLD banks (MCB, UBL, HBL) — global risk-off pressure offset by domestic flow stability. Position sizing: HALF-NORMAL until Brent direction confirms (a sustained move above $100 = bullish E&P; mean-reversion to $90 = neutralizes the trade). The trade has a SHORT shelf life — tactical 1-3 weeks, not strategic.
cases.what_breaks_it: (a) Rapid de-escalation / ceasefire — Brent collapses, all positions reverse. (b) Saudi/UAE supply increase announcement neutralises the geopolitical bid. (c) PSX-specific shock (IMF / political) overwhelms the global narrative. (d) US strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) release.
cases.research_basis: Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx — Sectoral Sensitivities (Oil & Gas / Refining). Empirical: Apr 2024, Oct 2024, May 2026 events (added 2026-05-11 strategist run).

reactions.narrative_note: US-Iran oil spike — protect E&P core positions but no sector-wide ADD (oil-spike-as-risk-off regime would neutralise the trade). OMC/Refining ceiling at HOLD.

cases.title: IMF review mission active in Islamabad (defensive posture, high binary risk)
cases.pattern: IMF review mission arrives in Pakistan for budget / SBA / EFF talks. The market enters a defensive holding pattern: turnover dries up (z-score -1 to -2), breadth narrows, banks / fertilizers / large caps outperform small caps. Outcome is binary: a successful conclusion (staff-level agreement) typically produces a 1-2% relief rally and PKR strength; a delay or hawkish conditionality produces a 1-3% sell-off. Foreign flows pause until the press conference. Most local PMs reduce gross exposure by 10-20% in the week leading up.

historical_instances.context: IMF first EFF review mission. Turnover z-score -1.8 in the 5d ahead. Outcome: staff-level agreement reached → +1.4% next session, PKR +0.3%.
IMF SBA-9 review. Conditionality dispute over PKR / power tariffs. Outcome: delay → -1.8% next session.

cases.playbook: Lower gross exposure to 30-40% of normal in the week BEFORE the mission. HOLD large-cap quality (MCB, UBL, FFC, OGDC) — these absorb a delay best. AVOID highly leveraged names (KAPCO, EPCL) and small caps (PABC, COLG) — they sell off most on a delay and lag on a relief rally. POST-OUTCOME: if SLA reached → BUY KSE-100 proxies + banks for a 5-10 day momentum window. If delay → wait for confirmation of a fresh mission date before re-entering. ALWAYS keep a +/-2% gap risk in mind on the morning after the press conference.
cases.what_breaks_it: (a) A pre-leaked outcome (PKR moves on Friday before the announcement) — the move is mostly priced in. (b) An external shock (political, geopolitical) overwhelms the IMF narrative. (c) IMF mission extended without a clear statement — the holding pattern persists and conviction degrades.
cases.research_basis: Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx — Macroeconomic Determinants (IMF / external account). Empirical: 2024-07 delay, 2025-09 success, 2026-05 (current — mission arrives May 15).

reactions.narrative_note: IMF mission active — moderate defensive. Cash 60%, Banks/Power -1 notch, half-size leveraged small-caps. (Calibrated 2026-05-13 backtest: prior settings -1.7% per fire on universe drift +0.4%.)

cases.title: Narrow breadth + low turnover → wait-and-see regime, no chasing
cases.pattern: Universe breadth (% of stocks up today) is below 30% AND PSX 60d turnover z-score is below -1. This combination signals a wait-and-see regime: institutional buyers have stepped back and retail conviction is low. Historically, returns over the next 5 days are MEAN-REVERTING (small positive drift +0.3-0.6%) but volatility is elevated and any single piece of news can produce a 1-2% gap. The classic mistake is to chase the few names that ARE up (often illiquid micro-caps) — they have the worst forward returns. Best play: hold quality, deploy ONLY in HIGH-conviction value+momentum setups (rare in this regime), keep 60-80% cash.

historical_instances.context: Breadth 22% positive, turnover z -1.6. KSE-100 drifted +0.4% over next 5 days, but breadth-led names (the few that were up) underperformed by ~2%.

cases.playbook: DO NOT chase the day's gainers — they have the worst forward returns in this regime. HOLD existing quality positions (MCB, UBL, OGDC, FFC, ATRL). DEPLOY new capital ONLY into setups that combine: HIGH quality + Phase-1 mom > 0 + value upside > 25% + a stock-specific catalyst (earnings beat, MF accumulation, volume confirmation). Keep gross exposure at 30-40%. AVOID adding to a losing position — mean-reversion in this regime is weak and slow.
cases.what_breaks_it: (a) A clear catalyst lands (rate decision, IMF outcome, large institutional flow) — regime shifts to either RISK-ON or CRISIS within 1-2 sessions. (b) Breadth recovers above 50% on rising turnover (z > -0.5) — the regime is over.
cases.research_basis: Empirical observation of PSX microstructure 2024-2026. The breadth+turnover combination is documented in 'Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx' — Behavioural Mechanics (institutional pause regimes).

reactions.narrative_note: Wait-and-see regime — DON'T chase day's gainers; hold quality.

cases.title: Universe sell-off >= -2% in 5d (mid-week safety net)
cases.pattern: KSE-100 / universe is down >= 2% over 5 trading days WITHOUT a single identifiable macro driver firing. Indicates broad de-risking — typically pre-event (IMF, MPS, results season open) or sympathy with global EM weakness. Conservative auto-overlay kicks in: raise cash floor, half-size all new BUYs, downgrade Banks/Cement/Power one notch.

historical_instances.context: PSX -3% Mon-Wed pre-IMF May 15. Banks -2.4%, Cement -3.7%, Power -3.0%, Pharma -3.9%. E&P held -0.9%.

cases.playbook: Mid-week safety net: lift cash floor to 70%, half-size all new BUYs, downgrade Banks/Cement/Power one notch. Keep E&P bias intact. Re-evaluate after the next macro print.
cases.what_breaks_it: If the sell-off is driven by a confirmed macro shock (rate hike, PKR collapse), defer to the more specific case (sbp_rate_hike_shock, pkr_devaluation_shock).
cases.research_basis: Empirical from May 11-13 post-mortem (saved 14 of 35 universe names from being held through -3% sector moves).

reactions.narrative_note: Universe broad sell-off (-4% 5d AND breadth <40%) — moderate defensive: cash 60%, Power -1 notch.

cases.title: Brent >= $100 absolute — cement margin pressure (coal/freight)
cases.pattern: Cement is leveraged to imported coal price + freight (both of which track Brent). When Brent breaches $100 absolute, north-zone Cement (DGKC, MLCF, KOHC) sees gross-margin compression of 200-400bps over 30-60 days. The PSX market typically lags this realisation by 5-15 sessions — there is a window to downgrade Cement BEFORE the market re-prices.

historical_instances.context: Brent $103 sustained — DGKC -4.2% / MLCF -3.8% over 21d.
historical_instances.source: PSX EOD + SBP Cement sector quarterly report Q4-2025.

cases.playbook: Downgrade Cement one notch on the strategist book. Particularly DGKC, MLCF, KOHC (north zone, coal-exposed). LUCK is least exposed (south zone, captive coal). Re-evaluate when Brent retraces below $95.
cases.what_breaks_it: (a) If domestic coal substitution accelerates, the linkage weakens. (b) If government caps electricity tariffs, margin pass-through is blocked.
cases.research_basis: Pakistan Stock Market Research Factors.docx — Sectoral Sensitivities (Cement / Imported Coal). Empirical Q4-2025 cycle.

reactions.narrative_note: Brent >= $100 — Cement margin squeeze; downgrade one notch.

cases.title: IMF mission active — soft defensive (any regime)
cases.pattern: IMF review mission is active in Pakistan but the universe regime is CAUTION or CRISIS (not NORMAL). The stricter `imf_review_mission_week` requires regime:NORMAL; this case covers the gap so we don't miss the IMF defensive lean simply because the regime classifier already moved off NORMAL.

historical_instances.context: IMF SBA mission active. Regime CAUTION at the time. Banks/Cement -2-3% in the lead-up week.

cases.playbook: Same direction as imf_review_mission_week but lighter overlay (don't double-trim). If imf_review_mission_week also fires, its larger overlays win on conflicts.
cases.what_breaks_it: Once the IMF outcome is known the binary collapses — exit defensive posture.
cases.research_basis: Same as imf_review_mission_week with widened regime gate.

reactions.narrative_note: IMF mission active (any regime) — moderate defensive. Cash 50%, Banks -1 notch.

cases.title: Nth rate cut (>=2nd) immediate-window mega-rally (days 1-14)
cases.pattern: When SBP delivers a cut that is NOT the first of the cycle AND the cut lands during a benign/normal regime, the first 1-14 trading days frequently produce an explosive broad rally (5-16% on the universe equal-weighted index). The mechanism: investors front-run the policy transmission, leveraged sectors re-rate immediately, and short-covering amplifies the move. Distinct from `sbp_rate_cut_cycle_initiation` (first cut only, n=1) and `post_cut_cycle_continuation` (starts at day 14). Catches the 14-day blind spot for later cuts (e.g. the 2025-05-15 +16.34% mega-rally on the 8th cut delivered 10 days prior). 2026-05-13 catalog: this single missing case was the largest source of uncaptured upside in the 5-year backtest.

historical_instances.context: 8th cut of cycle (12% -> 11%) on 2025-05-05; +16.34% universe / +21% Cement / +29% OMC over the next 5 trading days.
historical_instances.source: PSX EOD; SBP MPC press release 5-May-2025.

historical_instances.context: 5th consecutive cut (15% -> 13%) on 2024-12-16; sharp -6.82% drawdown then +9.40% over 21 days (V-bounce).
historical_instances.source: SBP MPC press release 16-Dec-2024; PSX EOD.

cases.playbook: ADD broadly across leveraged-cyclical sectors (Cement, Conglomerate/Chem, Power, OMC) with 14-day horizon. Avoid haircut on incumbent positions; let winners run. Don't re-fire `post_cut_cycle_continuation` overlays until day 14+. If the same week produces a sharp intraday drawdown, stay with the position — historical 21d return is positive even when day-1 is sharply red (see 2024-12-19 instance above).
cases.what_breaks_it: A simultaneous PKR shock or Brent spike (>=US$ 105) neutralises the rally. Also broken if regime flips to CRISIS during the window (broad cross-sectional capitulation).
cases.research_basis: 5-year per-sector catalog mining (2026-05-13). Of the top-10 universe-wide UP moves over 2021-2026, 6 fell into this window (later-cut, days 1-14, NORMAL regime). Only 1 of those 6 produced any case fire under the pre-Round-2 playbook.

reactions.narrative_note: Nth-rate-cut immediate window (days 1-14) — Banking-only sector upgrade (78% accuracy in 5y backtest, +2.08% vs-univ); leveraged sectors held at HOLD min (the broad-rally hypothesis only generalised to Banks).

cases.title: Oil spike PLUS broad sell-off (oil-up as systemic risk-off)
cases.pattern: When Brent spikes >=10% in 21d AND the universe equal-weighted index has fallen >=3% in 5 days AND breadth is below 30%, the oil rise is functioning as a SYSTEMIC RISK-OFF signal (geopolitical / inflation-shock / Fed hawkishness amplifier) rather than a pure commodity supply story. In this regime even E&P producers fall along with the broad market — the USD wellhead-pricing benefit is overwhelmed by the global risk-off discount applied to all PSX names. Discovered 2026-05-13 from per-sector mining: 2026-03-09 / 2026-03-17 / 2026-03-31 all had `oil_up:STRONG` AND broad capitulation; standard `brent_spike_e_and_p` UPGRADED E&P in those windows and was wrong direction (E&P -8.86% on 2026-03-17).

historical_instances.context: US-Iran tensions (event window) + IMF mission stress; Brent +12% / 21d; universe -5.93% / 5d; E&P -8.86% / 5d.

cases.playbook: DEFENSIVE posture: cash floor 60%, downgrade Cement / Banking / Power one notch (cyclicals get re-priced lower). Do NOT add E&P even though Brent is up — in this regime the wellhead-USD benefit is dominated by the systemic discount. Wait for breadth to recover above 40% before re-adding cyclical exposure. Hold cash 5-10 days; expect follow-on weakness for 2-3 weeks (21d return historically stays negative in this archetype).
cases.what_breaks_it: Brent reverses sharply or universe breadth recovers above 40% — the regime flips back to a normal `brent_spike_e_and_p` set-up where E&P can be added on the wellhead-USD trade.
cases.research_basis: Per-sector mining 2026-05-13: 3 of the top-10 universe-wide DOWN moves in 2026 (Mar 9/17/31) had `oil_up:STRONG` PLUS broad sell-off. Pre-Round-2 playbook fired E&P upgrade in all 3 — 0% accuracy.

reactions.narrative_note: Oil-spike-as-systemic-risk-off — defensive 5-10d. Cash 60%, cyclicals -1 notch. E&P NOT added (wellhead benefit dominated by systemic discount in this regime).

cases.title: Oil DOWN paired with broad EM equity sell-off
cases.pattern: Brent down >=5% / 21d while PSX universe is also -2% / 5d and breadth is sub-30%. This is the global recession-fear regime — oil falls because global demand expectations are deteriorating; PSX falls along with EM equities as foreign flows turn risk-off. Discovered 2026-05-13: dates with `oil_down:MODERATE` AND broad sell-off had no playbook case. (2021-11-26 -4.89%, 2023-05-10 -3.52%, 2022-04-05 -3.15%.) Pre-Round-2, only `behavioural_panic_3day` would fire — but that's behavioural, not macro.

historical_instances.context: Omicron-variant scare; oil -10% / week, PSX -4.89% / 5d, breadth 8%.

cases.playbook: MILD DEFENSIVE: cash floor 50%, downgrade Banking + Cement one notch (cyclicals exposed to global growth). DO NOT downgrade E&P even though oil is falling — Pakistan E&P has hedge contracts that smooth wellhead revenue across short oil moves. Hold 5 days. Re-evaluate if breadth recovers; the rebound from these is usually sharp (1-2 weeks once foreign flows stabilise).
cases.what_breaks_it: Oil reverses (supply shock) or breadth recovers above 40% — flip back to neutral cyclical posture.
cases.research_basis: Per-sector mining 2026-05-13. 4-6 dates over 2021-2025 with `oil_down:MODERATE` AND universe -2% / breadth low had no playbook case fire. Aggregate forward 5d (2026-05-13 catalog) was -1.8% on average.

reactions.narrative_note: Oil-down + broad sell-off (global demand-destruction regime) — defensive 5d; cash 50%, cyclicals -1 notch.
